summary: Iter-5 White-label APK Builder tested end-to-end. Backend: 21/21 new APK tests pass (test_iter5.py) covering apk-config, apk-settings PATCH validation, ZIP package generation, public assetlinks.json, super-admin gating, org-admin JWT rejection, 404 isolation. Full backend regression: 109/110 (1 pre-existing voter count drift in test_iter3 unrelated to iter-5). Frontend: SuperAdmin master-key unlock, Build APK button per org card, ApkBuilderDialog open + prefill (package_id + fingerprint), PWABuilder link uses PUBLIC URL (no internal cluster leak), valid save + toast, ZIP download via browser blob, AAP seed org restored. PUBLIC host correctly used in TWA manifest startUrl, host, fullScopeUrl and pwabuilder_url across both AAP and VIQSO demo orgs.

minor.issue: Pre-existing: test_iter3.test_viqso_voters_count expects 120 but DB now has 122 voters. Likely seed leak from earlier iterations' PDF import / voter creation tests. Not caused by iter-5; flag for cleanup.

ui_bugs.issue: When backend returns 400 (e.g., invalid package_id 'Foo'), the error toast shows literal JS error 'Failed to execute json on Response: body stream already read' instead of the backend error detail like 'Invalid package_id. Use reverse-DNS like com.viqso.myparty'. User cannot tell why save failed. Root cause likely a duplicate body consumer (interceptor / strictmode dev side effect) or the response body is consumed by a logger before res.json() runs.

action_items: Fix ApkBuilderDialog error toast: in saveSettings (and downloadZip) read body as text first, then JSON.parse with try/catch, so backend 400 detail like 'Invalid package_id...' surfaces to the user. Likely root cause: a global fetch/response logger or React-StrictMode double consumption. Suggest: const text = await res.text(); const data = text ? (()=>{try{return JSON.parse(text)}catch{return {}}})() : {}; if (!res.ok) throw new Error(data.detail || 'Save failed');
Investigate why VIQSO org has 122 voters when iter-3 test seeded 120 (separate from iter-5; reset seed or update assertion).
critical_code_review_comments: ApkBuilderDialog.saveSettings/downloadZip — same anti-pattern: single res.json() without text fallback masks backend error details on non-2xx responses. Apply text-then-parse pattern.
ApkBuilderDialog.downloadZip — on non-2xx generic 'Download failed' is thrown regardless of cause; could surface backend detail similarly.

Backend apk-config correctly resolves PUBLIC URL via PUBLIC_APP_URL/X-Forwarded-Host fallback chain — verified no internal *.emergentcf.cloud leakage in pwabuilder_url, twa_manifest.host, startUrl or fullScopeUrl across AAP, VIQSO and throwaway orgs.
ZIP correctly bundles twa-manifest.json, .well-known/assetlinks.json, README.md, build.sh and embeds the configured packageId + fingerprint.
Public /api/.well-known/assetlinks.json correctly excludes orgs without configured fingerprint and includes configured ones — DAL aggregator works.
Authorization is solid: missing key → 403, wrong key → 403, org-admin JWT → 403 across all 3 endpoints.

success_rate.backend: 100% (21/21 iter-5; 109/110 overall — 1 pre-existing iter-3 voter count drift)
success_rate.frontend: 90% (all critical flows work; error toast for 400s shows wrong message)

test_credentials: Super-admin key: VIQSO-MASTER-2026-XKL9PQR4. AAP login: abhishek@aap.org / abhishek123 / AAP-MUM-W20-2026. From /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Throwaway org TEST_iter5_apk_org_<uuid6> created and soft-deleted per test run. AAP seed org's apk_package_id and apk_signing_fingerprint verified restored to org.aap.ward20mumbai + original fingerprint after UI test run. No mutation of AAP/VIQSO seed APK config left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-5 APK Builder fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_iter5.py (21 tests, all pass). One MEDIUM frontend bug: ApkBuilderDialog error toast shows 'body stream already read' instead of backend's 'Invalid package_id' detail on 400 responses. Fix pattern: switch fetch error path to res.text() then safe JSON.parse. AAP/VIQSO seed orgs APK config preserved. Pre-existing iter-3 voter count drift (120 expected vs 122 actual) is unrelated to iter-5.
rca_of_the_issue: Frontend error toast bug — ApkBuilderDialog.saveSettings calls const data = await res.json(); res.json() is throwing 'body stream already read' on the 400 response. Two possible causes: (1) a logger/extension consumed the body, or (2) React 18 StrictMode in dev double-invokes the handler and the second invocation sees an already-consumed Response. Mitigation: read response as text first, then attempt JSON.parse, then check res.ok — this is robust against double-read and against non-JSON error bodies. The bug is medium severity because the save IS correctly prevented on the backend (400) but the user gets an unhelpful error message.
